subject: science
title: science_14.json
description: Grade 4 Science Assessment - Day 14: Electrical Circuits and Safety

questions.q: What component in an electrical circuit is designed to easily open or close the path of electricity?
questions.hint: You flip this on a wall to turn on the classroom lights.
questions.explanation: A switch controls the flow of electricity by interrupting the circuit path when open and completing it when closed.

questions.q: Why is household wiring usually wrapped in a layer of plastic or rubber?
options: To insulate the wires and prevent electrical shocks
To make the electricity travel much faster
To keep the copper metal cool so it does not melt
To generate extra power for the appliances
questions.answer: To insulate the wires and prevent electrical shocks
questions.hint: Plastic and rubber do not let electrical currents pass through them easily.
questions.explanation: Plastic and rubber are excellent insulators. They wrap around conductive wires to contain the electrical current safely.

questions.q: What source of stored chemical energy provides the voltage to push electrons through a portable circuit?
questions.hint: Flashlights, remote controls, and toys use them to get power.
questions.explanation: A battery converts stored chemical energy into electrical energy, acting as the power source in a direct-current circuit.

questions.q: What type of circuit has multiple separate pathways for electricity to flow, so that if one lightbulb burns out, the others stay lit?
questions.hint: Think about lines that run side-by-side next to each other, like tracks.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, components are connected along separate branches, allowing the current to bypass a broken component.

questions.q: If a circuit has a gap or a broken wire in its loop, it is called a(n):
questions.hint: Think about a drawbridge being raised; cars can no longer cross over it.
questions.explanation: An open circuit has a break or discontinuity in the path, which prevents electrons from flowing at all.

questions.q: What specific metal is most commonly used inside electrical wires because it is a fantastic conductor and bends easily?
questions.hint: It has a reddish-orange metallic color and is found inside power cords.
questions.explanation: Copper is highly conductive, relatively cheap, and flexible, making it the industry standard for electrical wiring.

questions.q: Which of the following is a dangerous practice when dealing with household electricity?
options: Yanking a power cord out of the wall by pulling on the wire instead of the plug
Using a plastic switch to turn off a desk lamp
Unplugging a toaster before cleaning inside it with a dry paper towel
Covering a wire gap with thick, heavy-duty electrical tape
questions.answer: Yanking a power cord out of the wall by pulling on the wire instead of the plug
questions.hint: This action can tear the internal wires loose from their safety coating.
questions.explanation: Pulling by the cord can tear the internal copper wires away from the plug contacts, creating a short-circuit or shock hazard.

questions.q: A safety device that melts and breaks a circuit if too much electricity flows through it is called a:
questions.hint: Its name sounds like it joined things together, but it actually breaks them to keep you safe from fires.
questions.explanation: A fuse contains a thin strip of metal designed to melt and open the circuit if the electrical current exceeds a safe level.

questions.q: Why is it dangerous to touch an electrical appliance or switch with completely wet hands?
options: Water with dissolved minerals conducts electricity, which can shock you.
Water makes the electricity travel too slowly to work properly.
Water dissolves the plastic casing around the household switch.
Water turns electrical energy into nuclear magnetic rays.
questions.answer: Water with dissolved minerals conducts electricity, which can shock you.
questions.hint: Think about how water acts as a bridge for an electric current to reach your body.
questions.explanation: Tap water contains impurities and minerals that allow it to act as an electrical conductor, increasing the risk of severe shocks.

questions.q: What do we call a path of low resistance that accidentally bypasses the normal load in a circuit, often causing it to overheat?
questions.hint: It takes a shortcut back to the battery, making things dangerously hot.
questions.explanation: A short circuit occurs when current flows down an unintended, low-resistance path, bypassing the appliance and causing excessive heat.
